source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

actions_taken: Floorhand immediately lowered to drill floor
Medevac helicopter called at 0430, arrived at 0550
Floorhand transported to hospital for emergency medical treatment

BSEE Houma District granted approval to resume operations on August 25, 2018 at 0450 with stipulation that personnel lifts using air hoists must have at least one spotter in direct communication with hoist operator
event.summary: On August 25, 2018 at 0345 hours, a Floorhand employed by Helmerich & Payne was severely injured while being hoisted in the derrick of rig H&P 107 to inspect the top drive assembly following a jarring operation. The Floorhand's secondary safety static line became snagged on a monkey board finger while he was being hoisted upward. The opposing forces created by the moving hoist line and the snagged safety line caused the Floorhand to be pulled in opposite directions, resulting in severe injury. The Toolpusher operating the man rider winch had obstructed line of sight due to racked drill pipe and was not using a flagger as required by the JSA and Pre-Job Checklist. The Floorhand was evacuated via helicopter and transported to hospital for emergency medical treatment.
recommendations: When JSA meetings take place, all personnel should speak up to address any issues with lines of sight
If lines of sight are an issue due to an obstacle, a second person should be given the responsibility of constantly monitoring the individual who is out of sight

Any personnel lift using air hoists must have at least one spotter depending on the need of the operation, and spotter(s) must be in direct communication with the hoist operator at all times while personnel are elevated

hazards.name: Personnel suspended at height during hoisting operations
Obstructed line of sight during winch operation

threats.name: Toolpusher unable to maintain visual contact with hoisted personnel due to drill pipe obstruction
Secondary safety static line becomes snagged on monkey board finger
Opposing directional forces applied to suspended personnel

consequences.name: Severe injury to Floorhand requiring emergency evacuation and hospitalization
Loss of work time and operational shutdown

controls.name: DBI Sala Bosun harness and man rider cable attachment
controls.barrier_role: Primary restraint system for personnel hoisting
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment for fall protection

supporting_text: The Floorhand prepared himself to be hoisted into the derrick by putting on a Duncan and Buck Industries (DBI) Sala Bosun harness and securing it to the man rider cable.

controls.name: Secondary safety static line with lifeline static cable grab
controls.barrier_role: Secondary restraint and fall arrest system

supporting_text: He also connected a three eighths inch secondary safety static line with a three foot lanyard and DBI Sala lifeline static cable grab to his set up.
the three-eighths inch safety static line became snagged on one of the fingers located on the monkey board
With the hoist line still moving upwards and the safety line snagged, there were forces created in opposite directions.

controls.name: Flagger to maintain line of sight during personnel hoisting
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure continuous visual monitoring of hoisted personnel
controls.lod_basis: Required by JSA and Pre-Job Checklist when line of sight is obstructed

supporting_text: It was discovered in the JSA and Pre-Job Checklist for Personnel Hoisting that if the line of sight is obstructed a flagger is to be used.
The BSEE team concluded that the Toolpusher's line of sight on the hoisted Floorhand was restricted and a flagger was not used.
The team confirmed that obstruction existed during the operation and a flagger should have been implemented as stated in the JSA and Pre-Job Checklist.

controls.name: Pre-tour safety meeting and JSA review
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and plan safe work procedures

supporting_text: At 1800 hours the night drilling crew would report to work but before reporting the crew would hold a 30 minute pre-tour safety meeting.
Prior to commencing work, the following documents were reviewed and signed by the crew: Permit to Work/Safe Work Permit, Pre-Job Checklist for Personnel Hoisting, and two Job Safety Analysis (JSA)

controls.name: Toolpusher visual monitoring of hoisted personnel
controls.barrier_role: Operational control requiring continuous visual contact between winch operator and hoisted personnel
controls.lod_basis: Standard operational procedure for safe hoisting

supporting_text: While the Toolpusher was operating the winch, he needed to reposition himself to the side of the winch due to the racked stands of drill pipe obstructing his line of sight.
The Toolpusher did not have a visual line of sight as the Floorhand was being pulled but he immediately stopped the winch once he heard the Floorhand screaming to stop.
He stated that he had to stand to the side of the drill pipe while operating the hoist in order to keep visual contact of the Floorhand due to the multiple stands of drill pipe obstructing the hoist.

controls.name: Emergency response and medical evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Mitigation control to minimize consequences of injury through rapid response and evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response and medical treatment procedures

supporting_text: A second Floorhand along with a Derrickhand immediately proceeded into the derrick to aid him.

The Floorhand was secured to a backboard and carried to await the medivac helicopter. The medevac helicopter was called at approximately 0430 arrived at 0550, and transported the IP to a hospital for further medical treatment.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
